data.title: CHAPTER 4 — CLAUDE CODE AS ENGINEERING PARTNER
blocks: A developer pastes a broken function into Claude chat. Claude produces a corrected version. The developer copies it back
Chapter 4 — Claude Code as Engineering P — This is the mismatch the chapter is about. Claude chat is the right tool for explaining code, discussing design options,

S02.meta: // SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · Explain the difference between Claude chat for code advice and Claude Code for codebase work
· Decide when a coding task needs repository context and a verification oracle
Describe the engineering workflow loop plan, permission, edit, run, inspect, decide
· Name the verification evidence for a coding task (tests, diffs, builds, lints)
· Understand what permissions control and why they matter
data.section: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — THE CORE CONCEPT: CODE REQUIRE

data.title: THE CORE CONCEPT: CODE REQUIRES A VERIFICATION ORACLE
blocks: The most important thing to understand about coding agents is not what they can do. It is how correctness gets establish
The Core Concept: Code Requires a Verifi — Code is not done when it is written. It is not done when it reads correctly. It is done when it passes external verifica

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — WHAT CLAUDE CODE ACTUALLY IS

data.title: WHAT CLAUDE CODE ACTUALLY IS.
blocks: · Read every file in your project
· Edit files and create new ones
· Run shell commands, builds, and tests
· Inspect test output and iterate
· Show diffs before writing

data.title: THE ENGINEERING PARTNER LOOP.
blocks: Define the issue.
Establish scope.
Review the plan.
Approve permissions.
Inspect the diff.

data.title: TASK ROUTING TABLE.
blocks: Explain a code snippet — Good fit
Fix a bug across multiple files — Weak fit
Generate pseudocode or design — Good fit
Run tests and inspect failure — Cannot access

S08.meta: // SLIDE 08 — WHAT CLAUDE CODE SHOULD NOT HA

data.title: WHAT CLAUDE CODE SHOULD NOT HANDLE ALONE.
blocks: · Design decisions. Whether the architecture is right for your system. Whether a refactor is worth the risk.
· Security review. AI-generated code may introduce vulnerabilities; security is a human responsibility with tools and proc
· Secrets management. Credentials, API keys, tokens, and sensitive configuration should not enter Claude Code's context at
· Production deployment decisions. Merging, deploying, and releasing require human accountability.
· Technical debt triage. Whether to fix now or defer, and what the downstream cost is, requires knowing the team, the road
data.section: WHAT CLAUDE CODE SHOULD NOT HA

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Treating Claude Code as Claude chat with more power.
Assuming compiled = correct.
Giving Claude Code a vague feature request.
Auto-approving permission prompts.
Skipping the diff.
